description:
You are helping a developer tidy up a small code base that lives in 
    /home/user/project.  
    The directory already exists and **already contains three files with the exact
    names and relative locations listed below**:

        /home/user/project/main.py
        /home/user/project/README.md
        /home/user/project/utils/helper.py   (note the sub-directory utils)

    Your job is to do three things:

    1. Compress the complete /home/user/project directory into a gzipped-tar
       archive whose absolute path is exactly  
       /home/user/project_backup.tar.gz

    2. Extract that archive so that an **identical copy** of the directory tree
       ends up at the absolute path  
       /home/user/project_backup  
       (i.e. after extraction we should have
       /home/user/project_backup/main.py,  
       /home/user/project_backup/README.md,  
       /home/user/project_backup/utils/helper.py).

    3. Produce a plain-text log file called /home/user/compression_log.txt that
       documents what you did.  
       The log file **must follow this exact five-line format** so that an
       automated test can read it:

            ARCHIVE_PATH:/home/user/project_backup.tar.gz
            EXTRACT_DIR:/home/user/project_backup
            FILES:
            /home/user/project/main.py;/home/user/project/README.md;/home/user/project/utils/helper.py
            STATUS:SUCCESS

       Notes on the format:
       • There are exactly five newline-terminated lines.  
       • The third line is literally the word “FILES:” (with the colon).  
       • The fourth line lists the three source-file paths separated by a single
         semicolon and **no spaces**.  
       • The fifth line must read exactly “STATUS:SUCCESS”.  
       • No extra blank lines are allowed at the beginning or end of the file.  

    The whole job can be done with just a couple of standard archive commands
    available on any Linux distribution (e.g. tar).  
    When you are finished, the verification script will check:

       • that /home/user/project_backup.tar.gz exists and is a valid gzip-tar;  
       • that /home/user/project_backup contains the three files in the correct
         directory structure and the file contents are byte-for-byte identical
         to the originals in /home/user/project;  
       • that /home/user/compression_log.txt exists and matches the five-line
         format given above, byte-for-byte.  

    Make sure to run all commands from the terminal and do not hard-code any
    paths other than the ones specified here.  
    Be mindful of letter-case: everything is lowercase exactly as written.
truth:
Initial filesystem state before the agent starts:

      Directories:
        /home/user
        /home/user/project
        /home/user/project/utils

      Files and their exact contents:

        /home/user/project/main.py
        --------------------------------
        print("Hello from main")
        --------------------------------

        /home/user/project/README.md
        --------------------------------
        # Project Sample
        A tiny project used for compression-task testing.
        --------------------------------

        /home/user/project/utils/helper.py
        --------------------------------
        def helper():
            return "helping"
        --------------------------------

    Required filesystem state after successful agent run:

      1. A file /home/user/project_backup.tar.gz
         • Must be a valid gzip-compressed tar archive that, when listed,
           shows a single top-level directory “project/” containing the
           three files with their original paths and byte-exact contents.

      2. A directory tree /home/user/project_backup/ with the following
         structure and byte-exact contents:

           /home/user/project_backup/main.py           (same content as original main.py)
           /home/user/project_backup/README.md         (same content as original README.md)
           /home/user/project_backup/utils/helper.py   (same content as original helper.py)

      3. A file /home/user/compression_log.txt whose exact content is:

            ARCHIVE_PATH:/home/user/project_backup.tar.gz
            EXTRACT_DIR:/home/user/project_backup
            FILES:
            /home/user/project/main.py;/home/user/project/README.md;/home/user/project/utils/helper.py
            STATUS:SUCCESS

         • The file must consist of exactly 5 lines (including the final newline).
